replies.msg: 不過佢地玩法唔係要高等教育精英制 所以無得拎啲成績直接同香港比較

同埋好似好多人唔知...，其實是人叻讀書好成績好而去外國讀書既人係少眾，大多都係香港讀唔到書屋企又有錢咪送去外國讀呃到大部分無知既人，其實有曬例子啲偽人啊議員啊啲假學歷都係外國比個錢就有
所以除非你係讀某幾間就係勁既外國大學，唔係根本現實講句就用錢買張證書呃下師奶，所以唔明點解有時連啲後生聽到人地係外國讀書就話人勁？我就幾時都覺得係香港讀到俾支那搶曬位既大學，唔洗做黑警，勁好多

replies.msg:
Dear Department of Accounting,

A penultimate year student at your university named Samantha Wong has violated the compulsory quarantine measures implemented by the Hong Kong government. It is in violation of Cap. 599E Compulsory Quarantine of Persons Arriving at Hong Kong from Foreign Places Regulation. Contravening the quarantine requirement would be a criminal offence. Offenders are subject to a maximum fine of $25,000 and imprisonment for six months.

Not only did Samantha intentionally violate such measures, her post on Instagram is extremely rude with no sense of remorse for her actions. Such selfish act could potentially put the community at risk so I hope you would take this seriously and not let her walk away with violating the law.

仲有寫email去accounting@lse.ac.uk 要求佢個dept嚴懲

Dear Department of Accounting,

A penultimate year student at your university named Samantha Wong has violated the compulsory quarantine measures implemented by the Hong Kong government. It is in violation of Cap. 599E Compulsory Quarantine of Persons Arriving at Hong Kong from Foreign Places Regulation. Contravening the quarantine requirement would be a criminal offence. Offenders are subject to a maximum fine of $25,000 and imprisonment for six months.

Not only did Samantha intentionally violate such measures, her post on Instagram is extremely rude with no sense of remorse for her actions. Such selfish act could potentially put the community at risk so I hope you would take this seriously and not let her walk away with violating the law.

replies.msg:
Dear Department of Accounting,

I am writing to infom you of reckless behaviour exhibited by one of your penultimate year student named Samantha Wong, who has violated the compulsory quarantine measures implemented by the Hong Kong government, puts the city at risk and proceed to brag about such ignorance on social media. Such behaviour is not only oblivious but also run against the fundamental trust from society and the people. Her actions are in clear violation of Cap. 599E Compulsory Quarantine of Persons Arriving at Hong Kong from Foreign Places Regulation. Contravening the quarantine requirement amounts to a criminal offence, an imprisonable offence. One would have thought that an LSE student is upright, honest and law abiding, however these characters are lacking from the aforementioned student, who highlights ignorance, no remorse and complete lack of moral judgement.

I trust the school will take actions to defend its traditions and values, to show the people what LSE stands for and what is right from wrong.

Sincerely
A concerned citizen

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am a Hong Konger, who is writing to complain about that a Hong Kong student of your accounting and finance programme (Year 2), namely Samantha Wong (or Samantha Wang), violated the Hong Kong government's self-quarantine policy.

According to her Instagram story, she went to the Wan Chai MTR station less than 14 days after she arrived in Hong Kong. Considering that the COVID-19, which has become pandemic, is extremely infective, this selfish, irresponsible behavior poses a great threat to all the Hong Kongers' health and safety. It also ruins the prestige of your department, as well as LSE as a whole.

I would very much appreciate it if you can take prompt disciplinary actions to show that such behavior cannot be tolerated. Attached are the evidence given by the netizens from the LIHKG forum in Hong Kong.

Yours faithfully,
Anonymous

quote.msg: 仲有寫email去accounting@lse.ac.uk 要求佢個dept嚴懲

Dear Department of Accounting,

A penultimate year student at your university named Samantha Wong has violated the compulsory quarantine measures implemented by the Hong Kong government. It is in violation of Cap. 599E Compulsory Quarantine of Persons Arriving at Hong Kong from Foreign Places Regulation. Contravening the quarantine requirement would be a criminal offence. Offenders are subject to a maximum fine of $25,000 and imprisonment for six months.

Not only did Samantha intentionally violate such measures, her post on Instagram is extremely rude with no sense of remorse for her actions. Such selfish act could potentially put the community at risk so I hope you would take this seriously and not let her walk away with violating the law.

Dear Department of Accounting,

I am writing to infom you of reckless behaviour exhibited by one of your penultimate year student named Samantha Wong, who has violated the compulsory quarantine measures implemented by the Hong Kong government, puts the city at risk and proceed to brag about such ignorance on social media. Such behaviour is not only oblivious but also run against the fundamental trust from society and the people. Her actions are in clear violation of Cap. 599E Compulsory Quarantine of Persons Arriving at Hong Kong from Foreign Places Regulation. Contravening the quarantine requirement amounts to a criminal offence, an imprisonable offence. One would have thought that an LSE student is upright, honest and law abiding, however these characters are lacking from the aforementioned student, who highlights ignorance, no remorse and complete lack of moral judgement.

I trust the school will take actions to defend its traditions and values, to show the people what LSE stands for and what is right from wrong.

Sincerely
A concerned citizen

quote.msg: 推去LSE
寫咗封投訴信俾LSE 大家幫幫手寄

應該甘講

First Class Honors = over 70/100

This student probably scored on average over 70 in her Year One courses so she put this in her LinkedIn, most students do this and it's not really misrepresentation so I think this paragraph is irrelevant and should be deleted from the email, otherwise it just makes you sound 多事

replies.msg:
you mis-understand the logic behind the honor
1. you CAN get first hon if your score surpasses overall 70 out of 100 AFTER final year
2. however, getting 70 out of 100 in first year does not mean you have the first hon - it means you are on track to get first hon if you did not fall below. It doesn't mean you have it.
3. Academic Qualification is strictly applied. E.g. PhD Candidate, the word candidate must be used unless and until you get pass the final defence and the conferment of the Doctorate degree.
a further example, you cannot claim you are a CFA charterholder, unless you pass all 3 levels with 2 years experience + receive the CFA title from the Institute.
4. Most students in the world don't do that, simply because it is not allowed. It may be your narrow view of what means 'most'
